channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hurricane Beryl rips through open waters after devastating the southeast Caribbean
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Hurricane Beryl roared through open waters on Tuesday as a monstrous Category 5 storm on a path that would take it near Jamaica and the Cayman Islands after earlier making landfall in the southeast Caribbean, killing at least four people.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea says new missile carries 'super-large warhead,' but experts skeptical of claims
item.description: North Korea claimed it test fired a missile that can carry “a super-large warhead." South Korea speculated the launch was actually unsuccessful and the North's report was a cover-up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian drone, hypersonic missile strikes escalate on Ukrainian air base ahead of arrival of F-16s
item.description: Starokostiantyniv, a small city in western Ukraine with a vital air base, has experienced an influx of Russian strikes due to the anticipated arrival of F-16 fighter aircraft.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hungarian prime minister arrives in Kyiv for Zelensky meeting
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban on Tuesday arrived in Kyiv, where he is set to meet with Ukrainian President Volodomyr Zelensky amid tensions between the two European leaders over Russia's war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Hungary's leader makes first visit to Ukraine since war began for peace talks with Zelenskyy
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán visited Kyiv for the first time since Russia's war with Ukraine began in February 2022 to meet with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Le Pen first had success in an ex-mining town. Her message there is now winning over French society
item.description: HENIN-BEAUMONT, France (AP) — In the former mining town at the heart of French far-right leader Marine Le Pen’s political strategy, her party's electoral success Sunday came as no surprise to hundreds of supporters who gathered to see her victory speech.
item.dc:creator: DIANE JEANTET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 9 dead after overnight strike in Gaza's Khan Younis following Israel's mass evacuation orders
item.description: At least nine people have been confirmed dead after an Israeli strike in the southern Gaza city of Khan Younis, according to Palestinian health officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN weather agency says Tropical Cyclone Freddy that hit eastern Africa last year was longest ever
item.description: The U.N. weather agency says it has confirmed that Tropical Cyclone Freddy, a deadly Indian Ocean storm that lashed eastern Africa last year, was the longest-lasting cyclone ever recorded — at 36 days.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dutch king swears in a new government 7 months after far-right party won elections
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The Netherlands has a different prime minister for the first time in 14 years as Dutch King Willem-Alexander swore in the country’s new government Tuesday, more than seven months after elections dominated by a far-right, anti-Islam party.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: José Raúl Mulino sworn in as Panama's new president, promises to stop migration through Darien Gap
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — José Raúl Mulino was sworn in Monday as Panama’s next president, facing pressure to slow irregular migration through the Darien Gap that connects his country with Colombia.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Driver whose car struck pedestrians in South Korea will face accidental homicide investigation
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A driver whose car struck pedestrians waiting at a crowded intersection in South Korea's capital, killing nine people, will be investigated on allegations of accidental homicide, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricane Beryl grows to Category 5 strength as it razes southeast Caribbean islands
item.description: BRIDGETOWN, Barbados (AP) — Hurricane Beryl strengthened to Category 5 status late Monday after it ripped doors, windows and roofs off homes across the southeastern Caribbean with devastating winds and storm surge fueled by the Atlantic's record warmth.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO and KOFI JONES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel orders Palestinians to flee Khan Younis, signaling likely new assault on southern Gaza city
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — The Israeli army ordered a mass evacuation of Palestinians from much of Khan Younis on Monday, a sign that troops are likely to launch a new ground assault into the Gaza Strip’s second-largest city.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, SAMY MAGDY and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tourist plane crashes onto highway in France, killing three: video
item.description: French authorities are investigating a plane crash that took the lives of at least three people. A tourist plane crashed on the side of the A4 highway near Collegien.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alleged Sinaloa cartel financial operator extradited to US
item.description: An alleged Sinaloa cartel “money man” will face American justice now that Mexico has extradited him to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, WGN 9

item.title: Trump Organization announces new project in Saudi Arabia
item.description: The Trump Organization will construct a Trump Tower building in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, the company announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Nick Robertson, The Hill

item.title: ADL sues Iran, North Korea and Syria over Hamas's Oct. 7 attack
item.description: The Anti-Defamation League (ADL) is going to court seeking to hold Iran, North Korea and Syria responsible for its role in aiding Hamas’s Oct. 7 attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Israeli plane refused fuel in Turkey after emergency landing: Report
item.description: Turkish officials have denied the claims, saying fuel was to be provided due to "humanitarian considerations.
item.dc:creator: Devan Markham, NewsNation

item.title: McLaughlin-Levrone sends pre-Olympic jolt, runs 50.65 to break world record (again) in 400 hurdles
item.description: EUGENE, Ore. (AP) — For the better part of two years, Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone all but disappeared from the 400-meter hurdles course. Turns out, she wasn't hiding or looking for something else to do. Just getting better at what she does best.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ultra-Orthodox protest against order to enlist in Israeli military turns violent in Jerusalem
item.description: Israeli police said protesters threw rocks and attacked the car of an ultra-Orthodox Cabinet minister.
item.dc:creator: OHAD ZWIGENBERG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: French far right scores convincing first-round election win: What to know
item.description: The far-right National Rally party in France scored a convincing early lead in the first round of the country’s parliamentary elections Sunday, according to pollsters, bringing controversial leader Marine Le Pen a step closer to forming a government.
item.dc:creator: Nick Robertson, The Hill

item.title: French voters propel far-right National Rally to strong lead in first-round legislative elections
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The far-right National Rally leaped into a strong lead Sunday in France's first round of legislative elections, polling agencies projected, bringing the party closer to being able to form a government in round two and dealing a major slap to centrist President Emmanuel Macron and his ri
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, LORI HINNANT AND SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Storms in Switzerland and Italy cause flooding and landslides, leaving at least 4 people dead
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Storms in Switzerland and northern Italy caused extensive flooding and landslides, leaving at least four people dead, authorities said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US struggles to deter Houthi threat as crisis spirals
item.description: After half a year of conflict, the U.S. has failed to deter the Houthi rebels from attacking merchant ships in the Red Sea as the Yemeni fighters continue to sink commercial boats and disrupt global trade, posing an increasingly difficult challenge for the far larger American military. Repeated U.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Twice-yearly injection fully protects women from HIV, trial finds
item.description: The trial showed the new injection "demonstrated 100% efficacy as a preventive treatment" for HIV among African women.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC
